xanesnet.models.dimenet package¶

Public API for the DimeNet and DimeNet++ models.

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.DimeNet(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, num_bilinear, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Directional message passing neural network (DimeNet).

Reference: “Directional Message Passing for Molecular Graphs”.

Implementation based on the PyTorch Geometric reference: https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/torch_geometric/nn/models/dimenet.html

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • out_channels (int) – Output feature dimension per atom.

  • num_blocks (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • num_bilinear (int) – Bilinear layer size in the interaction block.

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical basis functions; must be >= 2.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent controlling cutoff envelope smoothness.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual layers before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual layers after the skip connection.

  • num_output_layers (int) – Number of hidden layers in the output block.

  • act (str) – Activation function name (resolved via activation_resolver).

  • output_initializer (str) – Weight init for the final layer; one of "zeros" or "glorot_orthogonal".

__init__(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, num_bilinear, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Initialize DimeNet.

forward(z, edge_index, edge_weight, angle, idx_kj, idx_ji, batch=None)[source]¶

Compute per-atom output predictions.

Expects precomputed edges, distances, angles, and triplet indices as provided by GeometryGraphDataset with compute_angles=True.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (num_atoms,).

  • edge_index (Tensor) – Edge indices using j->i convention, shape (2, num_edges).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge distances in A, shape (num_edges,).

  • angle (Tensor) – Triplet angles in rad, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_kj (Tensor) – Index of the k->j edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_ji (Tensor) – Index of the j->i edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

  • batch (Optional[Tensor]) – Atom-to-sample assignments, shape (num_atoms,). Not used internally; accepted for interface compatibility.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom predictions of shape (num_atoms, out_channels).

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize model weights using DimeNet’s custom reset_parameters scheme.

The weights_init and bias_init arguments are ignored; DimeNet uses Glorot-orthogonal initialization internally via reset_parameters.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Ignored.

  • bias_init (str) – Ignored.

  • **kwargs – Ignored.

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return the model signature as a Config.

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.DimeNetPlusPlus(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, int_emb_size, basis_emb_size, out_emb_channels, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Bases: DimeNet

DimeNet++ improvement over the original DimeNet.

Reference: “Fast and Uncertainty-Aware Directional Message Passing for Non-Equilibrium Molecules”.

Implementation based on the PyTorch Geometric reference: https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/torch_geometric/nn/models/dimenet.html

Overrides the DimeNet output and interaction blocks with the DimeNet++ variants. The interaction block replaces the bilinear tensor product with separate basis projections, and the output block adds an up-projection before the final per-atom head.

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • out_channels (int) – Output feature dimension per atom.

  • num_blocks (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • int_emb_size (int) – Intermediate embedding size in the interaction block.

  • basis_emb_size (int) – Basis embedding size for RBF/SBF projection.

  • out_emb_channels (int) – Output embedding size before the final projection.

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical basis functions; must be >= 2.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent controlling envelope smoothness.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual layers before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual layers after the skip connection.

  • num_output_layers (int) – Number of hidden layers in the output block.

  • act (str) – Activation function name.

  • output_initializer (str) – Weight init for the final layer; one of "zeros" or "glorot_orthogonal".

__init__(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, int_emb_size, basis_emb_size, out_emb_channels, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Initialize DimeNetPlusPlus.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet module¶

DimeNet building blocks and spherical Bessel utilities shared with DimeNet++.

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.DimeNet(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, num_bilinear, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Directional message passing neural network (DimeNet).

Reference: “Directional Message Passing for Molecular Graphs”.

Implementation based on the PyTorch Geometric reference: https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/torch_geometric/nn/models/dimenet.html

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • out_channels (int) – Output feature dimension per atom.

  • num_blocks (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • num_bilinear (int) – Bilinear layer size in the interaction block.

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical basis functions; must be >= 2.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent controlling cutoff envelope smoothness.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual layers before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual layers after the skip connection.

  • num_output_layers (int) – Number of hidden layers in the output block.

  • act (str) – Activation function name (resolved via activation_resolver).

  • output_initializer (str) – Weight init for the final layer; one of "zeros" or "glorot_orthogonal".

__init__(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, num_bilinear, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Initialize DimeNet.

forward(z, edge_index, edge_weight, angle, idx_kj, idx_ji, batch=None)[source]¶

Compute per-atom output predictions.

Expects precomputed edges, distances, angles, and triplet indices as provided by GeometryGraphDataset with compute_angles=True.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (num_atoms,).

  • edge_index (Tensor) – Edge indices using j->i convention, shape (2, num_edges).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge distances in A, shape (num_edges,).

  • angle (Tensor) – Triplet angles in rad, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_kj (Tensor) – Index of the k->j edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_ji (Tensor) – Index of the j->i edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

  • batch (Optional[Tensor]) – Atom-to-sample assignments, shape (num_atoms,). Not used internally; accepted for interface compatibility.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom predictions of shape (num_atoms, out_channels).

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize model weights using DimeNet’s custom reset_parameters scheme.

The weights_init and bias_init arguments are ignored; DimeNet uses Glorot-orthogonal initialization internally via reset_parameters.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Ignored.

  • bias_init (str) – Ignored.

  • **kwargs – Ignored.

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return the model signature as a Config.

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.EmbeddingBlock(num_radial, hidden_channels, act)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Initial atom embedding block.

Maps atomic numbers to hidden representations and conditions them on the radial basis features of the incident edges. Atom-type embeddings are learnable and shared across all molecules.

Parameters:

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • hidden_channels (int) – Embedding and hidden dimension.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

__init__(num_radial, hidden_channels, act)[source]¶

Initialize EmbeddingBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x, rbf, i, j)[source]¶

Embed atomic numbers and condition on RBF edge features.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (num_atoms,).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Radial basis features for each edge, shape (num_edges, num_radial).

  • i (Tensor) – Destination atom index for each edge, shape (num_edges,).

  • j (Tensor) – Source atom index for each edge, shape (num_edges,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Edge-conditioned atom embeddings of shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.InteractionBlock(hidden_channels, num_bilinear, num_spherical, num_radial, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, act)[source]¶

Bases: Module

DimeNet bilinear interaction block.

Aggregates directional messages using bilinear mixing of RBF and spherical basis features, followed by residual layers and a skip connection.

Parameters:

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • num_bilinear (int) – Bilinear weight tensor size.

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical basis functions.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual layers before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual layers after the skip connection.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

__init__(hidden_channels, num_bilinear, num_spherical, num_radial, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, act)[source]¶

Initialize InteractionBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x, rbf, sbf, idx_kj, idx_ji)[source]¶

Compute updated message embeddings.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Message embeddings, shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Radial basis features, shape (num_edges, num_radial).

  • sbf (Tensor) – Spherical basis features, shape (num_triplets, num_spherical * num_radial).

  • idx_kj (Tensor) – Index of the k->j edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_ji (Tensor) – Index of the j->i edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated message embeddings of shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.ResidualLayer(hidden_channels, act)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Two-layer residual block with element-wise activation.

Parameters:

  • hidden_channels (int) – Input and output feature dimension.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

__init__(hidden_channels, act)[source]¶

Initialize ResidualLayer.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply residual transformation.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (*, hidden_channels).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Output features of shape (*, hidden_channels).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.OutputBlock(num_radial, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_layers, act, output_initializer='zeros')[source]¶

Bases: Module

Output block aggregating edge-level features into per-atom predictions.

Parameters:

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • out_channels (int) – Output dimension per atom.

  • num_layers (int) – Number of hidden linear layers.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

  • output_initializer (str) – Weight init for the final layer; one of "zeros" or "glorot_orthogonal".

__init__(num_radial, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_layers, act, output_initializer='zeros')[source]¶

Initialize OutputBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x, rbf, i, num_nodes=None)[source]¶

Aggregate edge features and project to per-atom predictions.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Edge message embeddings, shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Radial basis features, shape (num_edges, num_radial).

  • i (Tensor) – Destination atom index for each edge, shape (num_edges,).

  • num_nodes (int | None) – Total number of atoms (used for scatter output size).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom predictions of shape (num_nodes, out_channels).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.SphericalBasisLayer(num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff=5.0, envelope_exponent=5)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Combined radial-spherical basis encoding for bond-angle triplets.

Encodes triplet interactions using a joint Bessel-spherical basis (CBF): the radial part uses enveloped Bessel functions and the angular part uses real spherical harmonics. num_radial must be <= 64.

Parameters:

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical harmonics (max degree l). Must be >= 2.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial Bessel basis functions. Must be <= 64.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent controlling cutoff envelope smoothness.

__init__(num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff=5.0, envelope_exponent=5)[source]¶

Initialize SphericalBasisLayer.

forward(dist, angle, idx_kj)[source]¶

Compute combined Bessel-spherical basis features.

Parameters:

  • dist (Tensor) – Edge distances in A, shape (num_edges,).

  • angle (Tensor) – Triplet angles in rad, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_kj (Tensor) – Index of the k->j edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Combined basis features of shape (num_triplets, num_spherical * num_radial).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.BesselBasisLayer(num_radial, cutoff=5.0, envelope_exponent=5)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Learnable Bessel radial basis function layer with smooth cutoff envelope.

Encodes interatomic distances as num_radial enveloped Bessel basis values. The frequencies are learnable parameters initialized as pi * [1, 2, ..., num_radial] and smoothly decayed to zero at the cutoff by a polynomial envelope.

Parameters:

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • cutoff (float) – Cutoff distance in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent controlling envelope smoothness.

__init__(num_radial, cutoff=5.0, envelope_exponent=5)[source]¶

Initialize BesselBasisLayer.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Initialize learnable frequencies to pi * [1, 2, ..., num_radial].

Return type:

None

forward(dist)[source]¶

Encode distances as enveloped Bessel basis features.

Parameters:

dist (Tensor) – Interatomic distances in A, shape (num_edges,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Basis features of shape (num_edges, num_radial).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.Envelope(exponent)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Smooth polynomial envelope decaying to zero at x = 1.

Ensures Bessel basis functions and their derivatives are continuous and decay smoothly to zero at the cutoff boundary (x = dist / cutoff = 1). The polynomial degree is p = exponent + 1; coefficients a, b, c are derived from the smoothness conditions at the boundary.

Parameters:

exponent (int) – Controls smoothness; the effective polynomial degree is p = exponent + 1.

__init__(exponent)[source]¶

Initialize Envelope.

forward(x)[source]¶

Evaluate the envelope at normalized distances x = dist / cutoff.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Normalized distances in [0, 1), any shape (...).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Envelope values of shape (...); zero for x >= 1.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.Jn(r, n)[source]¶

Evaluate the n-th order spherical Bessel function at r.

Parameters:

  • r (float) – Radial coordinate.

  • n (int) – Spherical Bessel function order.

Return type:

floating[Any]

Returns:

Function value j_n(r).

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.Jn_zeros(n, k)[source]¶

Compute the first k zeros of each of the n lowest-order spherical Bessel functions.

Parameters:

  • n (int) – Number of spherical Bessel orders.

  • k (int) – Number of zeros per order.

Return type:

ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[single]]

Returns:

Array of shape (n, k) where [i, j] is the j-th zero of the i-th order spherical Bessel function.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.spherical_bessel_formulas(n)[source]¶

Return symbolic spherical Bessel function formulas for orders 0 to n-1.

Parameters:

n (int) – Number of spherical Bessel orders.

Return type:

list[Expr]

Returns:

List of n SymPy expressions in the symbol x.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.bessel_basis(n, k)[source]¶

Construct normalized Bessel basis function expressions.

Parameters:

  • n (int) – Number of spherical Bessel orders.

  • k (int) – Number of basis functions per order.

Return type:

list[list[Expr]]

Returns:

Nested list of shape (n, k) of SymPy expressions, where [i][j] is the j-th normalized Bessel basis function of order i.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.sph_harm_prefactor(k, m)[source]¶

Compute the real spherical harmonic prefactor for degree k and order m.

Parameters:

  • k (int) – Spherical harmonic degree.

  • m (int) – Spherical harmonic order.

Return type:

float

Returns:

Real spherical harmonic normalization prefactor.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.associated_legendre_polynomials(k, zero_m_only=True)[source]¶

Compute associated Legendre polynomial expressions up to degree k-1.

Uses the recurrence relations from: https://mathworld.wolfram.com/AssociatedLegendrePolynomial.html

Parameters:

  • k (int) – Maximum degree (exclusive); computes P_l^m for l in [0, k-1].

  • zero_m_only (bool) – If True, only compute m=0 polynomials.

Return type:

list[list[Any]]

Returns:

Nested list P_l_m where P_l_m[l][m] is a SymPy expression in the symbol z for the associated Legendre polynomial P_l^m.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet.real_sph_harm(k, zero_m_only=True, spherical_coordinates=True)[source]¶

Construct real spherical harmonic expressions up to degree k-1.

Parameters:

  • k (int) – Maximum degree (exclusive); computes Y_l^m for l in [0, k-1].

  • zero_m_only (bool) – If True, only compute m=0 harmonics.

  • spherical_coordinates (bool) – If True, express in spherical coordinates (theta, phi); otherwise use Cartesian (x, y, z).

Return type:

list[list[Any]]

Returns:

Nested list Y_func_l_m where Y_func_l_m[l][m] is a SymPy expression for the real spherical harmonic Y_l^m.

xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet_pp module¶

DimeNet++: fast directional message passing with reduced complexity.

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet_pp.DimeNetPlusPlus(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, int_emb_size, basis_emb_size, out_emb_channels, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Bases: DimeNet

DimeNet++ improvement over the original DimeNet.

Reference: “Fast and Uncertainty-Aware Directional Message Passing for Non-Equilibrium Molecules”.

Implementation based on the PyTorch Geometric reference: https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/torch_geometric/nn/models/dimenet.html

Overrides the DimeNet output and interaction blocks with the DimeNet++ variants. The interaction block replaces the bilinear tensor product with separate basis projections, and the output block adds an up-projection before the final per-atom head.

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • out_channels (int) – Output feature dimension per atom.

  • num_blocks (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • int_emb_size (int) – Intermediate embedding size in the interaction block.

  • basis_emb_size (int) – Basis embedding size for RBF/SBF projection.

  • out_emb_channels (int) – Output embedding size before the final projection.

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical basis functions; must be >= 2.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent controlling envelope smoothness.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual layers before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual layers after the skip connection.

  • num_output_layers (int) – Number of hidden layers in the output block.

  • act (str) – Activation function name.

  • output_initializer (str) – Weight init for the final layer; one of "zeros" or "glorot_orthogonal".

__init__(model_type, hidden_channels, out_channels, num_blocks, int_emb_size, basis_emb_size, out_emb_channels, num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_output_layers, act, output_initializer)[source]¶

Initialize DimeNetPlusPlus.

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet_pp.InteractionBlock(hidden_channels, int_emb_size, basis_emb_size, num_spherical, num_radial, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, act)[source]¶

Bases: Module

DimeNet++ interaction block.

Replaces the bilinear weight tensor from DimeNet with two separate linear projections for RBF and SBF features, reducing memory and computation.

Parameters:

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • int_emb_size (int) – Intermediate triplet embedding size.

  • basis_emb_size (int) – Projection size for RBF and SBF basis features.

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical basis functions.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual layers before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual layers after the skip connection.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

__init__(hidden_channels, int_emb_size, basis_emb_size, num_spherical, num_radial, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, act)[source]¶

Initialize InteractionBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x, rbf, sbf, idx_kj, idx_ji)[source]¶

Compute updated message embeddings.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Message embeddings, shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Radial basis features, shape (num_edges, num_radial).

  • sbf (Tensor) – Spherical basis features, shape (num_triplets, num_spherical * num_radial).

  • idx_kj (Tensor) – Index of the k->j edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

  • idx_ji (Tensor) – Index of the j->i edge for each triplet, shape (num_triplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated message embeddings of shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet_pp.ResidualLayer(hidden_channels, act)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Two-layer residual block with element-wise activation.

Parameters:

  • hidden_channels (int) – Input and output feature dimension.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

__init__(hidden_channels, act)[source]¶

Initialize ResidualLayer.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply residual transformation.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (*, hidden_channels).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Output features of shape (*, hidden_channels).

class xanesnet.models.dimenet.dimenet_pp.OutputBlock(num_radial, hidden_channels, out_emb_channels, out_channels, num_layers, act, output_initializer='zeros')[source]¶

Bases: Module

DimeNet++ output block with an additional up-projection layer.

Extends the DimeNet output block with an up-projection from hidden_channels to out_emb_channels before the final linear layers.

Parameters:

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • hidden_channels (int) – Hidden feature dimension.

  • out_emb_channels (int) – Output embedding dimension (up-projection target).

  • out_channels (int) – Output dimension per atom.

  • num_layers (int) – Number of hidden linear layers.

  • act (Callable) – Element-wise activation function.

  • output_initializer (str) – Weight init for the final layer; one of "zeros" or "glorot_orthogonal".

__init__(num_radial, hidden_channels, out_emb_channels, out_channels, num_layers, act, output_initializer='zeros')[source]¶

Initialize OutputBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Reset all learnable parameters to their initial distributions.

Return type:

None

forward(x, rbf, i, num_nodes=None)[source]¶

Aggregate edge features and project to per-atom predictions.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Edge message embeddings, shape (num_edges, hidden_channels).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Radial basis features, shape (num_edges, num_radial).

  • i (Tensor) – Destination atom index for each edge, shape (num_edges,).

  • num_nodes (int | None) – Total number of atoms (used for scatter output size).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom predictions of shape (num_nodes, out_channels).
